title: Augmented reality gaming systems and methods
abstract: An augmented reality (AR) game system is disclosed wherein real-world objects are transformed into AR terrain elements and AR events generate real-world impact. The game environment is set up using real-world objects that include everyday objects and game pieces on a field of play. When viewed on the screen of a computing device executing the modules of the game system, the everyday objects are transformed into elements of an AR terrain while the game pieces can be augmented with various controls. Multiple viewing modes, for example, a third person view, a first person view or "POV" view from a selected game piece or a split screen view are contemplated for viewing the field of play. A subset of the augmented controls can be activated by the user to execute AR events some of which can have real-world impact. The AR events are executed based on game rules. Detection mechanisms for detecting positions of game pieces within the field of play via their colors are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims claimed is: A method comprising: identifying, by a processor of a computing device, a field of play for placement of a plurality of physical objects; identifying, by the processor, physical game pieces among the plurality of physical objects placed within the field of play so as to differentiate between the game pieces and the field of play objects; visibly displaying, by the processor on a display screen of the computing device, the field of play as a terrain for an AR (Augmented Reality) game, wherein each of the field of play objects is displayed with a virtual appearance that differs from an appearance of the field of play object in the real world; determining, by the processor, if placements of the plurality of physical objects comport with game rules; providing feedback, by the processor, to a user of the computing device if the placements do not comport with the game rules; and signaling, by the processor, to the user that the AR game may be commenced upon sensing that the placement of the plurality of physical objects comport with the game rules. The method of claim 1, further comprising: augmenting, by the processor, an image of at least one of the physical game pieces shown on the display screen with at least one additional virtual control; and receiving, by the processor, user input to activate the additional virtual control. The method of claim 2, wherein the activation of the additional virtual control causes a physical change to the at least one physical game piece. 

 The method of claim 3, wherein the physical change comprises change in position of the at least one physical object from a first position to a second position, wherein the second position is in compliance with the game rules. The method of claim 2, further comprising: displaying, by the processor, a graphic animation upon the activation of the additional virtual control; and causing, by the processor, a physical change to another one the physical game pieces. The method of claim 5, wherein the other physical game piece is located in physical proximity to the at least one physical game piece. The method of claim 5, wherein the other physical game piece is located in a geographic location that is remote from a geographic location of the at least one physical game piece. The method of claim 1 wherein at least one of the physical objects placed in the field of play is an unknown object. The method of claim 8, further comprising: displaying, by the processor, the unknown object as an element of the terrain in the AR game. The method of claim 8, further comprising: instructing the user, by the processor, to replace the unknown object if the unknown object does not comport with the game rules. The method of claim 10, wherein an image of the unknown object is unchanged if the unknown object does not comport with the game rules. The method of claim 1 , wherein the physical game pieces are known to the processor via respective identifiers. 

 The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a signal from an new game piece for identification and inclusion within the field of play. The method of claim 13, further comprising: registering, by the processor, a respective identifier of the new game piece; and displaying, by the processor, an augmented image of the new game piece within the terrain of the AR game. The method of claim 1, further comprising: providing, by the processor, a plurality of options related to the AR game for user selection; receiving, by the processor, the user selection of one of the plurality of options; and overlaying, by the processor, the terrain associated with the user selected option on the field of play. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, by the processor, a user selection of one of the physical game pieces for generation of a point of view display; generating, by the processor, the point of view display of the terrain presenting positions of the physical game pieces in the field of play based on a perspective of the selected physical game piece; and displaying, by the processor, the point of view display on a screen of the computing device. The method of claim 1, further comprising: transmitting, by the processor, signal to affect an attribute of at least one of the physical game pieces at a predetermined time point in the AR game. The method of claim 17, wherein the signal is transmitted without user input. 

 19) The method of claim 1, wherein the field of play is identified with respect to the placements of the game pieces on a flexible mat. 20) The method of claim 19, wherein determining, by the processor, if placements of the plurality of physical objects comport with game rules further comprises: receiving, by another computing device comprised in the flexible mat, signals from the game pieces; and transmitting, by the other computing device, the signals received from the game pieces, to the processor. 21) The method of claim 1 wherein the computing device is a smartphone. 22) The method of claim 1 , wherein the computing device is a tablet. 23) The method of claim 1, wherein the computing device is a pair of AR glasses. 24) An apparatus comprising: at least one processor; a storage medium for tangibly storing thereon program logic for execution by the processor, the program logic comprising: field identifying logic, executed by the processor, for identifying a field of play for placement of a plurality of physical objects; game piece identifying logic, executed by the processor, for identifying physical game pieces among the plurality of physical objects placed within the field of play so as to differentiate between the game pieces and the field of play objects; displaying logic, executed by the processor, for visibly displaying on a display screen of the apparatus, the field of play as a terrain for an AR (Augmented Reality) game, wherein each of the field of play objects is displayed with a virtual appearance that differs from an appearance of the field of play object in the real world; 

 determining logic, executed by the processor, for determining if placements of the plurality of physical objects comport with game rules; feedback logic, executed by the processor, for providing feedback to a user of the apparatus if the placements do not comport with the game rules; and signaling logic, executed by the processor, for signaling to the user that the AR game may be commenced upon sensing that the placement of the plurality of physical objects comport with the game rules. The apparatus of claim 24, further comprising: augmenting logic, executed by the processor, for augmenting an image of at least one of the physical game pieces shown on the display screen with at least one additional virtual control; and input receiving logic, executed by the processor, for receiving user input to activate the additional virtual control, wherein the activation of the additional virtual control causes a physical change to the at least one physical game piece. The apparatus of claim 24, further comprising: animation logic, executed by the processor, for displaying a graphic animation upon the activation of the additional virtual control; and change logic, executed by the processor, for causing a physical change to another one of the physical game pieces. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein at least one of the physical objects placed in the field of play is an unknown object. The apparatus of claim 27 further comprising: logic executed by the processor for displaying the unknown object as an element of the terrain in the AR game. The apparatus of claim 28, further comprising: 

 instructing logic, executed by the processor, for instructing the user to replace the unknown object if the unknown object does not comport with the game rules. The apparatus of claim 26, further comprising: new game piece logic, executed by the processor, for receiving a signal from an new game piece for identification and inclusion within the field of play. The apparatus of claim 26, further comprising: game options providing logic, executed by the processor, for providing a plurality of options related to the AR game for user selection; option receiving logic, executed by the processor, for receiving the user selection of one of the plurality of options; and overlaying logic, executed by the processor, for overlaying the terrain associated with the user selected option on the field of play. The apparatus of claim 26, further comprising: point-of-view display generating logic, executed by the processor, for generating a point of view display of the terrain presenting positions of the physical game pieces in the field of play based on a perspective of a selected physical game piece and for displaying the point of view display on a screen of the computing device. The apparatus of claim 26, further comprising: logic for transmitting, by the processor, a signal to affect an attribute of at least one of the physical game pieces at a predetermined time point in the AR game.
